revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{Aimai}} '''埋土'''（うめど・うめつち・まいど）とは、 *地面の穴や窪地などを埋めた土を表す一般語。 *主に考古…」

Created page with "{{字引|date=2017年11月}} '''Umedo''' (埋土) refers to: * A general term for soil used to fill holes or depressions in the ground. * A term mainly used in {{ill|archaeology|ja|考古学}} to describe the soil deposited within {{ill|archaeological features|ja|遺構}}. It is also referred to as {{ill|fukudo (archaeology)|ja|覆土 (考古学)}}. In {{ill|archaeology|ja|考古学}} and {{ill|excavation surveys|ja|発掘調査}}, the two terms are used almost synonymo..."

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:埋土]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q56351956]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{字引|date=2017th year 11月}}
'''Umedo''' (埋土) refers to:

* A general term for soil used to fill holes or depressions in the ground.
* A term mainly used in [[:en:Archaeology|archaeology]] to describe the soil deposited within [[:en:Feature (archaeology)|Archaeological Feature]]. It is also referred to as {{ill|fukudo (archaeology)|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukudo (archaeology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}}. In [[:en:Archaeology|archaeology]] and [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation surveys]], the two terms are used almost synonymously. For example, in the reference literature listed below, it is described as "埋土（覆土）".{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs|2010|pp=94}}

== Notes ==

{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|language=ja|ref=harv|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|authorlink=Agency for Cultural Affairs|chapter=Chapter IV: Recognition of Soil Layers and Excavation of Surface and Inclusion Layers|title=Excavation Survey Guide: Settlement Site Excavation Edition|pages=93-116|date=2010-05|year=2010|publisher={{ill|Dōsei Publishing|ja|同成社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsei Publishing|qq=|1=Dōsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414650}}|isbn=9784886215253}}

{{disambiguation}}

[[Category:completion tier 1]]
{{translated page|ja|埋土|version=66339380|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Umedo''' (埋土) refers to:

* A general term for soil used to fill holes or depressions in the ground.
* A term mainly used in [[:en:Archaeology|archaeology]] to describe the soil deposited within [[:en:Feature (archaeology)|Archaeological Feature]]. It is also referred to as {{ill|fukudo (archaeology)|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukudo (archaeology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}}. In [[:en:Archaeology|archaeology]] and [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation surveys]], the two terms are used almost synonymously. For example, in the reference literature listed below, it is described as "埋土（覆土）".{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs|2010|pp=94}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:埋土]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:埋土]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q56351956]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{字引|date=2017th year 11月}}
'''Umedo''' (埋土) refers to:

* A general term for soil used to fill holes or depressions in the ground.
* A term mainly used in [[:en:Archaeology|archaeology]] to describe the soil deposited within [[:en:Feature (archaeology)|Archaeological Feature]]. It is also referred to as {{ill|fukudo (archaeology)|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukudo (archaeology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}}. In [[:en:Archaeology|archaeology]] and [[:en:Archaeological excavation|excavation surveys]], the two terms are used almost synonymously. For example, in the reference literature listed below, it is described as "埋土（覆土）".{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs|2010|pp=94}}

== Notes ==

{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* {{Cite book|language=ja|ref=harv|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|authorlink=Agency for Cultural Affairs|chapter=Chapter IV: Recognition of Soil Layers and Excavation of Surface and Inclusion Layers|title=Excavation Survey Guide: Settlement Site Excavation Edition|pages=93-116|date=2010-05|year=2010|publisher={{ill|Dōsei Publishing|ja|同成社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsei Publishing|1=Dōsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414650}}|isbn=9784886215253}}

{{disambiguation}}


{{translated page|ja|埋土|version=66339380|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:埋土]]

{{字引|date=2017th year 11月}}
'''Umedo''' (埋土) refers to:

* A general term for soil used to fill holes or depressions in the ground.
* A term mainly used in {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=archaeology|1=Archaeology}} to describe the soil deposited within {{ill|Feature (archaeology)|en|Feature (archaeology)|WD=Q814254|lt=Archaeological Feature|1=Feature (archaeology)}}. It is also referred to as {{ill|fukudo (archaeology)|ja|覆土 (考古学)|1=Overburden (archaeology)|lt=Overburden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukudo (archaeology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Overburden (archaeology)|qq=}}. In {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=archaeology|1=Archaeology}} and {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|WD=Q959782|lt=excavation surveys|1=Archaeological excavation}}, the two terms are used almost synonymously. For example, in the reference literature listed below, it is described as "埋土（覆土）".{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs|2010|pp=94}}
